explorer.influence: Angular body silhouette, dual humbucker layout, V-shaped lower bout, Tune-o-Matic bridge placement

klein.influence: Ergonomic negative-space cutaways, rear-access electronics, compact wiring, body-mounted pickups

upper_section.heritage: Explorer angular horn with Klein ergonomic relief

materials.notes: Prototype body is Khaya (African Mahogany) â€” warm tone, stable, easy to machine, ~0.50 g/cmÂ³. Production may use maple or ash depending on tonal goals.

body.weight_notes: Headless saves ~0.4 lb (no headstock/tuners). Klein cutaways remove ~0.6 lb of body wood. Electronics add back ~0.5 lb (Pi 5, Arduino, batteries, SSD, wiring). Net: comparable to a standard solid-body despite embedded computing.

cavities._design_notes: Hybrid approach: Explorer dual-humbucker front layout with Klein rear-access electronics. All cavity positions mapped to the STEM Guitar 24x32 grid (normalized 0-1 coordinates, origin top-left, Y increases downward).

neck_pocket.heritage: Standard â€” both Explorer (set-neck) and Klein (bolt-on) inform this; Smart Guitar uses bolt-on for serviceability

body_position_mm.notes: Centered on body axis, 53mm from top of body

bolt_pattern.notes: 4-bolt pattern for bolt-on neck. 44mm x 56mm spacing. Counterbored for flat-head screws.

neck_pocket.neck_angle_note: Bolt-on joint allows post-cut correction. Cut at 4.5deg, measure action on first article, shim 0.25-0.5deg if needed. Headless saddles provide additional +/-2mm action adjustment range.
neck_pocket.neck_angle_source: Explorer reference - conservative starting point for bolt-on headless. Adjust with pocket shim after first cut and string-up.
neck_pocket.op20_status: READY - cut at 4.5deg, verify on first article

neck_pickup_route.heritage: Explorer dual-humbucker layout â€” neck position

body_position_mm.notes: Converted from y_from_bridge=152.4mm. 167.6mm from top, 6 inches from bridge saddle, centered on axis

bridge_pickup_route.heritage: Explorer dual-humbucker layout â€” bridge position

body_position_mm.notes: Converted from y_from_bridge=25.4mm. 294.6mm from top, 1 inch from bridge saddle, centered on axis

bridge_route.heritage: Headless bridge with integrated fine tuners â€” Steinberger / Hipshot / ABM lineage. Replaces traditional TOM + tailpiece and headstock tuners. All tuning happens here.

dimensions_mm.notes: Headless bridge unit: saddles with individual fine tuners for each string. Mounts flat to body surface with 4 screws. No tailpiece needed â€” strings anchor and tune at bridge.

body_position_mm.notes: Scale length 628.65mm (24.75 inches, Gibson standard) measured from locking nut to bridge saddle contact point

rear_electronics_cavity.heritage: Klein rear-access electronics â€” no front control cavity. Pi 5 compute module housed in rear-routed chamber with main battery, SSD, and controls. Arduino ADC module separated near pickups (active-preamp style) with its own 9V battery

body_position_mm.notes: Offset right (bass side). Pi 5 only â€” 40% less wood removed vs dual-board cavity

cover_plate_screws.notes: 6mm inset from cavity edge, countersunk flush with cover plate

teensy_io_pocket.heritage: I/O coprocessor pocket — Teensy 4.1 handles footswitches, pots, LEDs, BMS telemetry, relay bypass. Audio path handled by Hi-Z USB interface and HiFiBerry HAT. Replaces Arduino Uno R4 (2026-03-25) — pocket 64% smaller, bare board 77% smaller.

contains.role: I/O coprocessor — footswitches, pots, LEDs, BMS telemetry, relay bypass. NO AUDIO.
contains.notes: Replaces Arduino Uno R4. Pocket area 64% reduction (4800→1750mm²); bare board 77% smaller. Same I/O role, bare-metal determinism.

signal_chain.path_b_digital: P90 → buffered_splitter → hi_z_usb_interface → raspberry_pi_5_usb → hifiberry_dac_adc_hat_i2s → headphone_out + wifi_pipewire_sink
signal_chain.wifi_output: PipeWire network sink via Pi 5 built-in WiFi — wireless monitoring to phone/tablet/DAW
signal_chain.teensy_power: 3.3V from Pi 5 USB — no independent battery needed
signal_chain.deprecated_path: arduino_adc_direct — 10-bit 44.1kHz inadequate for audio (Arduino Uno R4 removed 2026-03-25)

components.usb_interface: Focusrite Scarlett Solo 4th Gen (primary) or IK AXE IO ONE (alt)
components.pi: Raspberry Pi 5 — JACK + Guitarix/MODEP effects host
components.hifiberry: HiFiBerry DAC+ADC — I2S GPIO HAT hardware-clocked output

latency_targets_ms._note: DEPRECATED — see signal_chain.latency_ms for canonical values

teensy_io_pocket.independent_operation: Teensy 4.1 powered via USB from Pi 5 (3.3V). When Pi is off, relay bypass defaults to Path A dry output. No independent battery required.

structural_analysis.note: Pocket width reduced 60→25mm. Additional 35mm of lateral wood vs Arduino pocket.

grid_position.notes: Adjacent to neck pickup route, treble side. Short analog wire path to pickups

body_position_mm.notes: Near neck pickup (y_from_top ~155mm). Rear access via small 2-screw plate. Teensy powered via USB from Pi 5.

cover_plate_screws.notes: 2-screw pattern on long axis. 5mm inset from cover edge.

teensy_io_pocket.function_note: Houses Teensy 4.1 — I/O coprocessor only. Audio handled by Hi-Z USB interface and HiFiBerry HAT.

rear_wiring_channel.heritage: Klein rear-wiring philosophy â€” all pickup leads run through rear-routed channels, no front wiring trenches

paths.name: pickups_to_teensy
paths.route_description: Short analog lead from pickup cavities to Teensy GPIO, ~20mm

paths.name: teensy_to_pi5_usb_serial
paths.route_description: USB serial cable from Teensy to Pi 5 via rear channel, runs alongside pickup wiring, ~140mm

paths.name: bridge_pickup_to_teensy
paths.route_description: Bridge pickup lead routed up to Teensy I/O pocket via rear channel, ~130mm

paths.name: output_jack_channel
paths.route_description: Channel from Pi 5 cavity to jack plate on lower bout edge, ~75mm

output_jack.heritage: Explorer-style side-mount barrel jack on lower bout

dimensions_mm.angle_notes: 15 deg inward angle toward body center for comfortable cable routing

grid_position.notes: Bass-side lower bout edge, accessible when playing

body_position_mm.notes: Angled bore through lower bout edge

usb_c_port.heritage: Modern edge-mount port â€” Strandberg / Lava ME style side-access charging

dimensions_mm.notes: Rectangular slot accommodates USB-C receptacle with waterproof gasket. Routed from body edge inward.

grid_position.notes: Bass-side body edge, same Y-band as rear electronics cavity â€” short internal cable to Pi 5 USB-C. Below arm contour, won't interfere with playing.

body_position_mm.notes: Edge-routed slot perpendicular to body surface. Body half-width ~219mm at this Y â€” slot opens at edge, extends 7mm inward. ~60mm cable to Pi 5.

usb_c_port.function: USB-C PD 20W charging for Li-ion 18650 + optional USB data connection to Pi 5

antenna_recess.heritage: IoT best practice â€” PCB antenna under thin wood window, invisible from outside. Used in smart furniture, acoustic wireless systems (e.g., Taylor ES2), and IoT enclosures.

geometry_clarification.description: Stepped shelf milled into floor of rear_electronics_cavity
geometry_clarification.notes: The 2mm RF window is the thin wood between antenna pocket and rear cavity. The 20.45mm is structural floor to front face. Antenna sees 2mm wood, not 20.45mm.

grid_position.zone: BODY_CANVAS (rear face, upper region of electronics cavity)
grid_position.notes: Centered near top of rear cavity area. Maximizes distance from copper shielding paint in pickup routes. Rear face only â€” invisible from front.

body_position_mm.notes: Rear face of body. Milled from inside the rear electronics cavity as a stepped shelf â€” no separate external routing needed.

antenna_recess.connection: U.FL pigtail to Pi 5 M.2 WiFi module
antenna_recess.signal_notes: 2mm wood cover attenuates ~1-3dB at 2.4GHz, acceptable for room-scale range (10-15m). No external hardware visible.

control_plate_surface.heritage: Hybrid â€” Explorer top-mount controls simplified with Klein minimalism. 2 knobs + 1 rotary switch mounted on asymmetric plate, no deep front cavity needed

grid_position.notes: Below and right of bridge, accessible control cluster

control_plate_surface.rear_cavity_note: Pot shafts extend through body to control_cavity. Wiring harness housed in rear pocket.

body_position_mm.notes: Derived from grid_position (x=0.65, y=0.78). 55.2mm right of centerline, 346.7mm from top

control_cavity.heritage: Les Paul control cavity scaled for 3-knob layout — LP heritage 108×64×31.75mm scaled to 100×60×20mm. Houses pots, switch, wiring harness. Accessed via rear cover plate.

body_position_mm.notes: Aligned with control_plate_surface (x=55.2mm, y=346.7mm). Rear pocket directly behind front-mounted controls.

cover_plate.notes: Flush-mount cover with #4 × 1/2 inch wood screws. 5mm margin around cavity.

cnc_operations.roughing: OP-CC-50: 6mm flat endmill, adaptive clearing, 3mm stepdown
cnc_operations.finishing: OP-CC-51: 6mm flat endmill, contour pass, 0.5mm stepover
cnc_operations.cover_recess: OP-CC-52: 6mm flat endmill, 3.175mm deep pocket for cover plate
cnc_operations.drilling: OP-CC-60: 2.5mm drill, 4× screw pilot holes, 10mm deep

wiring_channels.notes: Channels drilled from control cavity floor to connect pickup routes and jack cavity

neck.nut_notes: Headless locking nut â€” strings clamped at nut end, tuned at bridge. No headstock, no tuning pegs.
neck.headless_notes: Headless design reduces overall instrument length by ~180mm. Strings pass through locking nut to headless bridge with integrated fine tuners. Shorter neck-behind-nut stub for string clamping.

neck_length.note: protrusion + tenon = blank. Protrusion = overall_length - body_length. Blank minimum equals body length exactly.

bridge.notes: Headless bridge unit â€” all tuning at bridge. Locking nut clamps strings at neck end.

smart_features.notes: Separated dual-board architecture: Pi 5 in main rear cavity (95x65x22mm) with Li-ion battery, Teensy 4.1 near pickups (70x25x20mm). If Pi 5 is off, Teensy relay bypass defaults to Path A dry output. Rear cavity oversized for future smart modules (Bluetooth, MIDI, preamp)

software_stack.description: Software components that run on the Pi 5 Music Brain for practice coaching, loop generation, and session bundles.

sg_agentd.description: FastAPI agent daemon wrapping zt_band for HTTP-accessible practice sessions

endpoints.description: Generate a new clip bundle from parameters
Regenerate a clip with modified parameters
Retrieve a clip bundle by ID

zt_band.description: Zone-Tritone Band — jazz practice pattern engine with Markov chains and tritone zone theory

components.bundle_writer: src/zt_band/bundle_writer.py — writes clip bundles to disk

bundle_format.description: Canonical clip bundle structure for practice sessions

string_master.description: Parent project containing zt_band and shared zone-tritone theory

luthiers_toolbox_integration.notes: ToolBox acts as HTTP proxy to sg-agentd running on Pi 5 (or localhost for dev)

stem_grid_mapping._description: All cavity positions mapped to the STEM Guitar 24x32 grid system. Grid origin is top-left, coordinates normalized 0-1. The grid centerline is at x=0.5. This mapping enables the GridZoneClassifier to validate cavity placement against standard guitar anatomy zones.

zone_usage.BRIDGE_ZONE: bridge_route (headless bridge with integrated fine tuners)
zone_usage.WAIST_LEFT: occupied by Klein-style upper void (negative space)

stem_grid_mapping.routing_philosophy: Klein rear-access: no front control cavity. All wiring in rear-routed channels. Front surface has only pickup rings, bridge hardware, and control plate mounting holes

overall.notes: Headless design â€” no headstock. Overall length is body (368.3mm) + neck extension to nut (~315mm) + short clamping stub (~30mm). ~180mm shorter than headed equivalent.

notes: First mockup - dimensions are approximate estimates from visual analysis
Grid reference image used for proportional scaling
Explorer-influenced angularity with Klein-style negative space cutaways
Natural finish softens aggressive angular aesthetics
v1.1 (2026-03-08): Added cavity and wiring specs derived from Explorer + Klein hybrid analysis
Cavity positions mapped to STEM Guitar 24x32 grid for vectorizer integration
Explorer contribution: body shape, dual HB layout, V lower bout
Klein contribution: ergonomic voids, rear-access electronics, no front control cavity, minimal front routing
Headless design: no headstock, locking nut, tuning at headless bridge. Gibson 24.75 scale with 24 frets.
